metadata_information.title: The performance of decentralized school systems : evidence from Fe y Alegría in Venezuela
metadata_information.version: Python script, from D&R API, generated on 2026-03-19

title_statement.title: The performance of decentralized school systems : evidence from Fe y Alegría in Venezuela

document_description.abstract: This program evaluation estimates the effects on standardized test scores of graduating from the Fe y Alegría private school system in Venezuela. The authors find an Average Treatment Effect on the order of 0.1 standard deviations (approximately 16 percent of the average score), using a control group of public school students. These effects are significantly larger for households at the bottom of the distribution, and smaller for those at the top. The authors posit that the better performance of the Fe y Alegría system stems from their labor contract flexibility and decentralized administrative structure.
